title: Berliner Unterwelten: Quick Summary
items: **Berliner Unterwelten is a guided-tour association, not a walk-in museum.** Every site is entered on a fixed 90 minute tour with one of their guides. Public tours cost €18, or €13 reduced.

**Tickets are online only and appear about 30 days ahead.** There is no ticket desk at the meeting point, group sizes are limited, and advance tickets cannot be exchanged or refunded. Set a reminder for the day your date opens.

**The tours do not all start at Gesundbrunnen.** Tour 1 meets at Brunnenstraße 105 by the station. The escape tunnel tour meets at Brunnenstraße 141, down at Bernauer Straße, two kilometres and four U8 stops away. Same street, different city.
**If you only do one, do Tour 1, Dark Worlds.** A wartime civilian air-raid bunker under Gesundbrunnen station, plus about 160 years of what Berlin built under its own streets.

**Go to Bernauer Straße instead if the Wall is your reason for coming.** That tour ends at the only original escape tunnel under the Wall you can still see, and the free Berlin Wall Memorial is across the road.

**The rules are strict and they are the thing that catches people.** No photography anywhere, nothing bigger than a small daypack, sturdy shoes obligatory, around 10°C all year, stairs and no lifts, no toilets on site, and nobody under 7.
**The flak tower tour is the exception on ages and season.** Volkspark Humboldthain, 18 and over only, April to October, helmets issued.
